text: Found {mundane_item}. {hoarder_justification}
Found a {water_item}. Current count: indeterminate. Packing it anyway.
Spotted medical supplies in a pouch labeled PROBABLY CLEAN.
Found a shield recharger in a crate labeled ELECTRICAL MAYBE. That's either useful or a personality test.
An Anxietick {robot_flavor_anxietick}. Hid behind a crate. It made a thoughtful little lunge at ankle height.
An Anxietick {robot_flavor_anxietick}. Panicked, sprinted, and introduced itself to every nearby object at speed.
An Anxietick {robot_flavor_anxietick}. It bolted toward a catastrophic workplace-safety incident that had your name on it.
A Tattletale drone {robot_flavor_tattletale}. Relocated at speed.
A Tattletale drone {robot_flavor_tattletale}. Its broadcast brought immediate and extremely persuasive consequences.
A Tattletale drone {robot_flavor_tattletale}. Entire zone joined the conversation. Medical review pending.
Roomba Prime {robot_flavor_roomba_prime}. It tried to take significant pieces of you. The floor was very clean.
Roomba Prime {robot_flavor_roomba_prime}. It pursued with the confidence of a machine that has never known mercy.
Roomba Prime {robot_flavor_roomba_prime}. Initiated full clean-up cycle. Handler is advised to begin paperwork.
A Bomber Who Misreads the Room {robot_flavor_bomber_misread}. Everyone involved suddenly remembered they had somewhere else to be.
Found a locker and hid in it. A robot is also waiting outside. We're both very patient.
Met another raider. We emoted at each other for several minutes then both ran in opposite directions.
Formed a temporary alliance with another raider. We split up immediately. Classic.
Spotted a crate in the distance. It is definitely full of good things and not a robot.
Something metallic rummaged nearby. Raider investigated because survival instincts were apparently out of office.
Tripped on a rock. The rock won.
Stepped on a twig. A Tattletale drone swiveled toward me instantly.
Thought I saw a crate. It was an Anxietick pretending to be a crate. It panicked first.
Teammate sneezed loudly. A Tattletale drone immediately broadcast our location.
Saw loot in the distance. It was too far. Emotionally and physically.
Heard a nervous whirring behind me. An Anxietick froze midâ€‘skitter.
Teammate grabbed the loot I was reaching for. I pretended not to care.
A Tattletale drone scanned me from head to toe. I failed whatever test it was running.
Opened a crate. It fell apart like my hopes.
Saw a shadow before the robot. It was a Crusher of Dreams. Terrifying.
Teammate sprinted past me screaming. I followed without asking questions.
A Tattletale drone pinged loudly. I pinged back. It judged me.
Saw glowing loot. Ran toward it. Immediately regretted running.
Heard heavy footsteps. Not mine. Not good. Definitely a Roomba Prime.
Teammate confidently led us the wrong way. We followed anyway.
A Tattletale drone beeped aggressively at me. I beeped back. Mistake.
Loot crate exploded when I touched it. I pretended that was normal.
A Tattletale drone spotted me instantly. I was standing completely still.
Teammate pinged loot. It was a rock. They insisted it looked shiny.
A Tattletale drone activated a siren. I activated panic.
Saw loot behind a Bomber Who Misreads the Room. Considered my options. None were good.
A Walker Texas Malfunction turned toward me very slowly. Too slowly.
Teammate fell off a ledge. I pretended not to laugh.
A Bomber Who Misreads the Room ambushed us from behind a crate. I screamed internally.
Loot was placed in the open. Too open. Definitely bait.
A laser dot appeared on my chest. Roomba Prime was nearby. I chose not to investigate.
Teammate and I raced for loot. They won. I pretended I let them.
A Walker Texas Malfunction sparked violently. I took that personally.
Saw loot underwater. Decided drowning wasnâ€™t worth it. Barely.
Heard a robot making a breathing noise. It was an Anxietick. Somehow worse.
Teammate yelled 'RUN!' without context. I ran anyway.
A Crusher of Dreams stared at me for a full five seconds. I blinked first.
Loot was just out of reach. I stretched. I failed.
A Tattletale drone triggered an alarm. I triggered despair.
Teammate dropped loot while running. I considered picking it up. Considered.
A Roomba Prime appeared behind me silently. Too silently.
Loot was stuck in a tree. I stared at it helplessly.
A Tattletale drone emitted a warning beep. I emitted a warning scream.
Teammate pinged danger. It was a bush.
A Roomba Prime flew overhead. I froze like a malfunctioning toaster.
An Overthinker Tick {robot_flavor_overthinker_tick}. Eventually chose attack. Eventually.
An Overthinker Tick {robot_flavor_overthinker_tick}. Considered every option. Chose all of them simultaneously.
A Passive-Aggressor Drone {robot_flavor_passive_aggressor}. Did not fire. The judgment was worse.
A Seeker of Validation {robot_flavor_seeker_validation}. It hesitated mid-charge, waiting for feedback. I did not provide any.
A Harvester of Mild Annoyances {robot_flavor_harvester_annoyance}. Blocked every useful path while loudly collecting nothing.
An Enforcer of Minor Inconveniences {robot_flavor_enforcer_inconvenience}. Technically following protocol. Technically.
An Enforcer of Minor Inconveniences deployed suppressive fire. Mostly at the wrong targets. Including me.
A Receipt Printer of Doom {robot_flavor_receipt_printer_doom}. It cited me for loitering near value.
A Receipt Printer of Doom jammed, screamed, and weaponized the paperwork backlog.
An Apology Turret {robot_flavor_apology_turret}. Very sorry. Very armed.
An Apology Turret asked me to hold still for a courtesy burst. I declined. It escalated politely.
A Sniper of Poor Decisions {robot_flavor_sniper_poor_decisions}. It waited until I stopped moving. Classic.
A Sniper of Poor Decisions missed me. Once. It did not miss again.
A Tank of Overcompensation {robot_flavor_tank_overcompensation}. It announced the attack several times. Still terrifying.
A Tank of Overcompensation revved its engine unnecessarily before charging. The floor vibrated. So did I.
Daylight revealed a safe-looking supply nook. Suspiciously normal. Looted it with one eye on the exit.
Night patrol lights swept the street. Every robot suddenly looked like it had coffee and a promotion to chase.
Found a black-box cache humming in the dark. It was valuable in the way warning labels are valuable.
Overtime Red painted the loot crate like an after-hours bonus. Opened it. Heard distant machinery clock in.
Overtime Red turned the streetlights hostile and something pinged my position like unpaid labor.
Found a field cache with one battery, two lies, and three receipts. Filed all of it under loot.
Supply drop landed in a puddle with perfect comedic timing. Loot still mostly loot.
Found strange markings that clearly meant STOP TOUCHING CRATES. Took a rubbing for later ignoring.
Broken water pipe sprayed heroically. Filled the canteen, the boots, and several regrets.
Old security turnstile demanded a badge. I vaulted it and felt briefly employed.
Abandoned grow trays contained one surviving lettuce. It looked disappointed in my loadout.
Peeled a warning label off a machine. Under it: a second warning label with better handwriting.
Abandoned kiosk asked me to rate the raid experience. Selected 'in progress' and stole the pen.
Opened a crate and found a smaller decoy crate inside. The smaller crate looked smug.
Found a key labeled TURNKEY. No door nearby. Packed it because destiny loves clutter.
Broken escalator claimed to be stairs. I accepted its truth and climbed with resentment.
Apology Weather apologized into the comms, then made the stairs slippery anyway.
Polite Glyphs suggested I stop opening crates. I appreciated the tone and ignored the message.
A Tremor of Bad Priorities moved the exit farther away and a crate closer. Nature has opinions.
A humming crate migrated into the open and waited for me to develop character flaws.
Found an OopsAll Logistics crate addressed to ELSEWHERE. Took responsibility for the delivery chain.
A cracked HushTone receiver whispered static with the confidence of a secret. Packed it for evidence.
Found an Exit Strategy Unlimited route card. It labeled the nearest wall as OPTION B.
Passed a damaged dam still labeled CIVIC CONFIDENCE PROJECT. It leaked water and several promises.
Buried retail district had an escalator pointing at a half-open store kiosk. Terrible route. Excellent crate smell.
Old transit board said all departures were delayed, not cancelled. I admire the commitment to denial.
Soggy tower stairs led up to top-floor loot and down to several physics concerns. I chose up because I am unwell.
Found a warning-sign museum. Every sign said DO NOT. I took the smallest one for morale.
Found a launch manifest clipboard under rubble. Every name was crossed out except WAITLIST. Took it as a sign.
Found an emergency snack shrine arranged around a tiny warning label. Took the trial mix. Left a bolt as tribute.
Found a questionable field cache packed with exactly the kind of loot that makes people ignore footsteps.
Found a Staycation tent with a decoy crate outside and three suspiciously patient footprints inside.
A raider in a locker whispered that camping is just patience with furniture. I hate how prepared that sounded.
Found a half-zipped apparel duffel making bold claims about pockets. Investigating for science and pockets.
Found a weapon-parts bin labeled SORTED. The contents are conducting a metallic group argument.
Found a lost-and-found shelf. Nothing has names on it anymore, which feels like permission wearing a fake mustache.
Raider is arguing with the backpack zipper like it has legal standing.
Raider reports the robot is vulnerable to confidence. The robot is not reacting like a stakeholder.
Raider is making small talk with the extraction timer. The timer remains professionally distant.
Raider has filed a floor report. Initial findings: cold, dusty, judgmental.
